headline: How Higher Fuel Costs Are Rewriting The Delta Air Lines (DAL) Investment Story
summary: Delta Air Lines is seeing a modest reset in its valuation work, with one fair value estimate moving slightly from US$81.29 to US$80.57 per share. Analysts are largely tying this kind of trim to higher fuel cost assumptions, even as they debate how demand strength and premium positioning might balance the risk and reward. As you read on, you will see how these shifting price targets fit into a broader narrative and what to watch next as views evolve. Stay updated as the Fair Value for Delta...

("Delta"), a global leader in power management and smart green solutions, is showcasing at NVIDIA GTC 2026 its latest spectrum of energy-saving power, liquid cooling, and microgrid solutions engineered specifically for the 800 VDC architecture of next-generation AI factories. headline: Stocks to Watch Today: Uber, Hyundai, Delta, Lululemon
summary: ↗️ Uber Technology (UBER): The ride-hailing company’s stock jumped nearly 4% ahead of the opening bell after Uber and Nvidia said Monday they were expanding their self-driving-vehicle partnership. ↗️ Hyundai Motor (KR:005380), Kia (KR:000270), Nissan Motor (JP:7201), Isuzu Motors (JP: 7202), Geely Automotive (HK:175): Shares of the Asian auto makers all moved higher after Nvidia announced Monday it was expanding its autonomous-driving partnership with them.

headline: Delta Stock Is Surging. Why It’s a Pivotal Day for Airlines.
summary: Delta Air Lines stock jumped ahead of the open Tuesday as the carrier stuck to its earnings guidance—and even hiked its revenue outlook despite surging fuel prices.  Well they will now hear it from the horse’s mouth as the biggest U.S. carriers are set to provide updates at the JPMorgan Industrials Conference in Washington.  Delta shared its presentation ahead of time in a filing early Tuesday.

headline: Nvidia to power Uber autonomous cars, Oklo's nuclear agreement with DOE
summary: Market Catalysts Host Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Markets and Data Editor Jared Blikre track several of the day's top trending stock tickers, including American (AAL) and Delta Air Lines (DAL) forecasting strong bookings, Uber's (UBER) plans to roll-out autonomous vehicles powered by Nvidia (NVDA), and Oklo (OKLO) signing an agreement with the US Department of Energy.

headline: Oil prices climb as fresh strikes target infrastructure
summary: Oil prices rose again Tuesday as Iran launched fresh attacks on crude-producing neighbours, while stock markets were higher ahead of key central bank meetings.   Australia's central bank hiked its key interest rate Tuesday, pointing to "sharply higher fuel prices".

content.title: Oil prices climb as fresh strikes target infrastructure
content.summary: Oil prices rose again Tuesday as Iran launched fresh attacks on crude-producing neighbours, while stock markets were higher ahead of key central bank meetings.   Australia's central bank hiked its key interest rate Tuesday, pointing to "sharply higher fuel prices".

headline: Stocks Are Rising as Oil Prices Pull Back From Highs
summary: Stocks opened higher on Tuesday as oil prices eased from their overnight highs, and big airline stocks took flight.  The Nasdaq Composite was up 0.6%.  Futures were actually lower earlier in the morning, but West Texas Intermediate crude oil futures pulled back from about $98.42 overnight.

Futures were actually lower earlier in the morning, but West Texas Intermediate crude oil futures pulled back from about $98.42 overnight.

headline: These Stocks Are Today’s Movers: Nvidia, Oklo, Delta, Uber, Honeywell, Elbit, and More
summary: Stock futures were rising Tuesday, extending gains from Monday.  CEO Jensen Huang said at the event that Nvidia expects to sell at least $1 trillion worth of Blackwell and Rubin chips from 2025 through to the end of 2027.  The ride-hailing company and Nvidia expanded their partnership on self-driving vehicles, with Uber pledging to launch an expansive fleet of robo-taxis enabled by Nvidia’s DRIVE platform by 2028.

content.title: These Stocks Are Today’s Movers: Nvidia, Oklo, Delta, Uber, Honeywell, Elbit, and More
content.summary: Stock futures were rising Tuesday, extending gains from Monday.  CEO Jensen Huang said at the event that Nvidia expects to sell at least $1 trillion worth of Blackwell and Rubin chips from 2025 through to the end of 2027.  The ride-hailing company and Nvidia expanded their partnership on self-driving vehicles, with Uber pledging to launch an expansive fleet of robo-taxis enabled by Nvidia’s DRIVE platform by 2028.

headline: Delta's Lifted Forecast Leads Airline Stocks Higher
summary: Delta Air Lines says conflict in the Middle East hasn’t cracked air-travel demand yet, pulling its shares and other airline stocks higher in premarket trading after a miserable month for the sector.  The carrier raised its revenue outlook for the first quarter, citing healthy demand for corporate and leisure travel, both internationally and domestically.  “We're seeing strength in every market that we look at,” CEO Ed Bastian said at the JPMorgan Industrials Conference on Tuesday morning.

headline: Delta and American Airlines Stocks Jump. Why They Hiked Revenue Guidance Despite Iran War.
summary: Delta Air Lines  and  American Airlines Group  stocks jumped early Tuesday as the carriers stuck to their earnings guidance—and even hiked revenue outlooks—despite surging fuel prices.  Delta shared its presentation ahead of time in a filing early Tuesday.  The carrier said it expects its first-quarter guidance to be within its initial range—earnings per share of 50 cents to 90 cents.

Delta shared its presentation ahead of time in a filing early Tuesday.  The carrier said it expects its first-quarter guidance to be within its initial range—earnings per share of 50 cents to 90 cents.

headline: Airlines Seeing Sturdy Demand for Flights
summary: Airlines say sturdy demand for flights is helping them ride out climbing jet fuel costs tied to the war in Iran.  Delta Air Lines and American Airlines raised their revenue outlooks on Tuesday to account for strong bookings in the first three months of the year, despite conflict erupting in the Middle East and a streak of severe winter weather disruptions.  Delta CEO Ed Bastian said demand has been healthy for corporate and leisure travel, both internationally and domestically.

content.title: Airlines Seeing Sturdy Demand for Flights
content.summary: Airlines say sturdy demand for flights is helping them ride out climbing jet fuel costs tied to the war in Iran.  Delta Air Lines and American Airlines raised their revenue outlooks on Tuesday to account for strong bookings in the first three months of the year, despite conflict erupting in the Middle East and a streak of severe winter weather disruptions.  Delta CEO Ed Bastian said demand has been healthy for corporate and leisure travel, both internationally and domestically.
